title: Add /bin/sh -c to entrypoint in config
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Prepend ''/bin/sh -c" to the entrypoint value given by the user for the `buildah config` command.  This addresses #593 

Tests:
```
# ctr=$(buildah from centos:7)
# buildah config --workingdir /tmp $ctr
# buildah config --entrypoint /bin/bash $ctr

# buildah commit $ctr test1
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:43e653f84b79ba52711b0f726ff5a7fd1162ae9df4be76ca1de8370b8bbf9bb0
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:4f4fb700ef54461cfa02571ae0db9a0dc1e0cdb5577484a6d75e68dc38e8acc1
Copying config sha256:ae0382211cbf19f667ef1b3bbcc97b4195ebc1d5119813ca90cdad78eba83371
 1.24 KiB / 1.24 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

# buildah from test1
test1-working-container

# buildah run test1-working-container
[root@mrsdalloway tmp]# 
```

comments.body: Can you add a test to verify the problem that this is fixing?
@rhatdan maybe on the tests, will have to play with how to verify that it worked OK.  Looking into it.

the buildah run tests aren't happy with this change.  For instance line 83 in run.bats is failing as the value being returned is " " rather than "pwd".  Trying to figure out what's what, but am also spending more time on the BZ that I has with docker ps.  Hope to wrap the bz early this afternoon, then chase the run tests down.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably e008b73) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@rhatdan @nalind PTAL.  I've messed up my sandbox.  If this is OK I'd like to merge this and I'll add the requested test in  a separate PR.
Latest patch updates the nit by @rhatdan and added the requested test for @ripcurld
